concept.conceptName: Bicarbonate | Blood mixed venous | Chemistry - non-challenge
Bicarbonate | Blood central venous | Chemistry - non-challenge
Bicarbonate | Serum or Plasma | Chemistry - non-challenge

en.name: Bicarbonate in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Bicarbonate (HCO3⁻) is the main extracellular buffer and a key component of acid-base balance [1]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a biochemistry analyser, a blood gas analyser, or a point-of-care device. Results are reported as a substance concentration in mmol/L (SI unit) or mEq/L; the two are numerically equivalent for bicarbonate [2]. This concept set aggregates bicarbonate measurements across blood specimens (serum, plasma, arterial, venous, capillary, mixed venous, central venous and unspecified blood). Cord blood specimens are excluded — they reflect the foetal-placental circulation at birth and are not physiologically equivalent to post-natal blood from the same neonate. **Note: actual vs. standard bicarbonate.** LOINC distinguishes two forms: - **Actual bicarbonate** — calculated from the patient's measured pH and PaCO2 at the time of sampling. - **Standard bicarbonate** — recalculated as if PaCO2 were 40 mmHg at 37 °C, to isolate the metabolic component independently of the respiratory status. In the absence of respiratory disturbance the two values are nearly identical, but they can diverge substantially in respiratory acidosis or alkalosis. **Both forms are included in this concept set** because most clinical sources and studies aggregate them under "bicarbonate". Study designs that explicitly compare actual vs. standard bicarbonate should preserve the distinction at the source value level. ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on nine LOINC Hierarchy concepts (one per blood specimen, scoped to `Chemistry - non-challenge`), with their descendants pulled in. It resolves to 13 LOINC "Lab Test" concepts, grouped here by specimen. ### Serum or plasma (default specimen for routine biochemistry) - *3016293 / 1963-8 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: most widely used LOINC code worldwide. - *42868453 / 69964-5 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] standard in Plasma (LOINC)*: standard form. - *40757490 / 54359-5 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] in Plasma --post dialysis (LOINC)*: point-in-time measurement immediately after a haemodialysis session. - *3050416 / 48631-6 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] in Serum --post dialysis (LOINC)*: point-in-time measurement immediately after a haemodialysis session. ### Generic blood (specimen unspecified) - *3006576 / 1959-6 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] in Blood (LOINC)* ### Arterial blood - *3008152 / 1960-4 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] in Arterial blood (LOINC)*: typically reported by a blood gas analyser. - *3014218 / 19230-2 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] standard in Arterial blood (LOINC)*: standard form. ### Venous blood - *3027273 / 14627-4 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] in Venous blood (LOINC)* - *3007628 / 19232-8 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] standard in Venous blood (LOINC)*: standard form. ### Central venous blood - *1616794 / 97543-3 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] in Central venous blood (LOINC)*: from a central venous catheter. - *1617100 / 97544-1 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] standard in Central venous blood (LOINC)*: standard form. ### Mixed venous blood - *3025817 / 19229-4 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] in Mixed venous blood (LOINC)*: typically from a Swan-Ganz catheter. - *3006538 / 19233-6 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] standard in Mixed venous blood (LOINC)*: standard form. ### Capillary blood - *3015235 / 1961-2 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] in Capillary blood (LOINC)*: capillary heel-prick or fingerstick (neonatal, point-of-care). - *3004490 / 19231-0 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] standard in Capillary blood (LOINC)*: standard form. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`mmol/L`** (concept 8753 — millimole per litre). Source systems may report `mmol/L` or `mEq/L`; for bicarbonate (a monovalent anion) the two are numerically equal. ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded by name. The set deliberately omits the following LOINC concepts: - **Cord blood specimens** (e.g. `3037749 / 14151-5 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] in Cord blood`, `3017809 / 28640-1 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] in Arterial cord blood`, `3018650 / 28641-9 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] in Venous cord blood`, plus their `standard` variants): physiologically distinct from post-natal blood (foetal-placental circulation at birth). - **Red Blood Cells specimens**: intra-erythrocytic bicarbonate, specialised research measurement. - **Body fluid, CSF, dialysis fluid, peritoneal fluid, pleural fluid, amniotic fluid, water, nonbiological fluid specimens**: out of scope for a "blood" concept set. - **Urine specimens**: out of scope (captured by the separate "Urinary bicarbonate" concept set). ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "bicarbonate", "HCO3", "total CO2", "tCO2" without further specification: `3016293 / 1963-8 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma`. By far the most common LOINC code. - **Source reports a bicarbonate value from a blood gas analyser** (typically arterial): map to `3008152 / 1960-4 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] in Arterial blood`. - **Source explicitly labels the value as "standard bicarbonate"** (or "sHCO3", "sBic"): map to the corresponding `standard` LOINC code for the specimen. Do **not** merge with the actual bicarbonate without checking the study design — the two values can diverge in respiratory disturbances. - **Do not confuse with**: urinary bicarbonate (separate concept set), base excess (different measurand), carbon dioxide partial pressure (PaCO2 — different measurand, separate concept set). ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 1963-8 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma. Available: https://loinc.org/1963-8/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
